headline: GOLD HITS $4,878 — Fourth Straight Weekly Gain as US-Iran Ceasefire Buoys Metals Complex
snippet: Gold climbed to $4,878/oz on April 17, up 1.65% on the day, as a 10-day US-Iran ceasefire kept the Strait of Hormuz open and eased inflationary fears. The metal is up 42% year-over-year and on pace for its fourth consecutive weekly gain — still below the January all-time high of $5,589 but clearly breaking out of a mid-quarter consolidation.

headline: POTENTIAL URANIUM DISCOVERY: Cosa Resources Hits 13,900 CPS at Murphy Lake North, Athabasca Basin
snippet: Cosa Resources' 2026 winter drill program at Murphy Lake North in Saskatchewan's Athabasca Basin intersected 5.0 metres of anomalous radioactivity up to 13,900 counts per second (CPS) approximately 260 metres from surface. Core photos indicate visible uranium mineralization — a potential new discovery in the basin; assay results are expected in Q2 2026.

headline: SILVER INSTITUTE 2026 SURVEY: 67Moz Deficit Forecast — Sixth Straight Annual Shortfall Confirmed
snippet: The Silver Institute's annual World Silver Survey, released April 15, confirmed record silver prices in 2025 driven by lease rate spikes and physical tightness. The 2026 forecast projects a 67 million ounce structural deficit — the sixth consecutive annual shortfall — as industrial and investor demand outpaces a forecast 1% supply increase to a decade-high 1.05 billion ounces.

headline: COPPER BREAKS $6.10/LB — Two-Month High as US-Iran Diplomacy Revives Industrial Metal Demand
snippet: Copper futures climbed to $6.10/lb on April 16, hitting two-month highs and fully recovering losses since the start of the Middle East conflict as Washington and Tehran prepared a second round of ceasefire talks. Analysis highlights a structural 10-million-tonne annual demand gap expected by 2040 from EV, AI data-centre, and grid electrification buildouts — which is beginning to reprice junior copper developers.

headline: NevGold Hits Consistent Antimony-Gold at Nevada Leach Pads: 0.34% Sb and 0.41 g/t Au Over 12.5m
snippet: NevGold Corp. confirmed high, consistent antimony and gold grades across 10 of 17 drill holes on historic leach pads at its Limousine Butte Project in Nevada, including 0.34% antimony and 0.41 g/t gold over 12.5 metres. A maiden antimony-gold resource estimate is targeted for Q2 2026 with near-term production possible by 2027 — a rare domestic antimony source as China controls over 60% of global supply.

headline: GOLD-SILVER RATIO PEAKED AT 105: GoldSeek Sees Historic Reversal Setting Up Silver Catch-Up Rally
snippet: The gold-to-silver ratio peaked above 105 in April before beginning to contract — a pattern that has historically preceded sharp silver outperformance in every prior precious metals cycle. GoldSeek's SWOT analysis argues that normalization toward the long-run average of ~60 implies silver in the $40–$50 range if gold holds near current levels, offering leveraged upside for silver mining stocks.

headline: West Red Lake Gold Drills 215.46 g/t Au Over 5.35m at Madsen Mine — 904 Complex Unmined Until Now
snippet: West Red Lake Gold Mines reported bonanza-grade results from the Austin 904 Complex at its Madsen Mine in Ontario's Red Lake Gold District, including 215.46 g/t gold over 5.35 metres and 388.90 g/t over 2.95 metres. The 904 Complex is a previously unmined section of the deposit and will be the primary drill focus throughout 2026; initial production is targeted for H1 2027.

headline: AbraSilver Drills 171m at 0.83 g/t Au at Diablillos Phase VI — Oculto East Expands Beyond Current Resource
snippet: AbraSilver Resource's Phase VI drill program at its Diablillos project in Argentina returned 171 metres grading 0.83 g/t gold and 10.3 g/t silver from 230 metres depth, including a higher-grade 9-metre interval at 3.46 g/t gold. The broad Oculto East zone continues to grow beyond the existing open-pit mineral resource at one of the Western Hemisphere's largest undeveloped silver-gold deposits.

headline: K92 Mining Q1 2026: 46,743 oz AuEq With Record Lateral Development — Stage 3 Expansion on Track
snippet: K92 Mining produced 46,743 oz AuEq (44,022 oz gold, 1.7M lbs copper, 38,845 oz silver) in Q1 2026 from its Kainantu Mine in Papua New Guinea, in line with budget, with ore throughput up 37%. Record quarterly mine development of 3,007 metres exceeded Stage 3 expansion requirements; full-year guidance of 190,000–225,000 oz AuEq is maintained with H2 weighting.

headline: China Sulfuric Acid Export Ban Could Throttle Silver Supply — 70% of Mine Output at Risk: Money Metals
snippet: Money Metals Exchange reports that China has signaled a ban on sulfuric acid exports potentially through end-2026 — a supply threat that has received little mainstream coverage. Roughly 70% of annual silver mine production is a copper byproduct that requires sulfuric acid processing, meaning the ban would compound an already deeply deficit silver market.

headline: Asian Metals: Gold and Silver Extend Gains, Geopolitical Premium Returns — GoldSeek Market Update
snippet: GoldSeek's April 14 Asian metals market update tracks the return of safe-haven buying and geopolitical risk premium to gold and silver as Middle East tensions persisted. Asian physical demand remains elevated, with open interest in gold futures at multi-month highs and retail buying in both China and India still robust — supporting floor prices heading into the week.
